question_text:
Course Schedule II

There are a total of numCourses courses you have to take, labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1. You are given an array prerequisites where prerequisites[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that you must take course bi first to take ai. Return the ordering of courses you should take to finish all courses. If there are many valid answers, return any of them. If it is impossible, return an empty array.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= numCourses <= 2000
  • 0 <= prerequisites.length <= numCourses * (numCourses - 1)
  • All pairs prerequisites[i] are distinct.
output_format: An array representing a valid course order, or an empty array if impossible.
explanation:
Kahn's Algorithm (BFS Topological Sort)

Build adjacency list and in-degree array. Use a queue initialized with all nodes of in-degree 0. Dequeue, add to result, decrement neighbors' in-degrees; enqueue any that reach 0. If result length equals numCourses, a valid ordering exists.

Time: O(V+E). Space: O(V+E).
